title: Support volumeMounts.subPath in podman kube play
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

Please consider adding support for `volumeMounts.subPath` in `podman kube play`.
This topic was briefly discussed in 2020 (#5654) but the request was rejected at the time.
I would like to request it again to improve compatibility between kubernetes and podman.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create `/tmp/my-lamp-site.yaml` with this content:
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: my-lamp-site
spec:
    containers:
    - name: mysql
      image: docker.io/library/mysql
      env:
      - name: MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD
        value: "rootpasswd"
      volumeMounts:
      - mountPath: /var/lib/mysql
        name: site-data
        subPath: mysql
    - name: php
      image: docker.io/library/php:7.0-apache
      volumeMounts:
      - mountPath: /var/www/html
        name: site-data
        subPath: html
    volumes:
    - name: site-data
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: my-lamp-site-data
```

2. Create a volume : `podman volume create my-lamp-site-data`

3. `podman play kube /tmp/my-lamp-site.yaml`

**Describe the results you received:**

mysql's data is written at the root of the `my-lamp-site-data` volume

**Describe the results you expected:**

mysql's data should have been stored in a subfolder named `mysql` inside the volume because that's what `subPath` points to.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      4.0.0-dev
API Version:  4.0.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.17.5
Built:        Fri Jan 14 16:29:25 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

wait @fpoirotte so you are just looking for the `mountPath` and the `subPath` to be joined in the container?

``subPath`` refers to a path inside the host's volume (rather than inside the container's mount point).

Given we have a named volume ``myvolume`` defined on the host with the following contents:
* website/
  * index.html
* mysql/
  * binlog.000001
  * somedb/
    * sometable.MYD
    * sometable.MYI
    * ...
  * ...

Now suppose we run podman play kube with the following manifest:
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: my-lamp-website
spec:
    containers:
    - name: php
      image: docker.io/library/php:7.0-apache
      volumeMounts:
      - mountPath: /var/www/html
        name: website-data
        subPath: website
    volumes:
    - name: website-data
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: myvolume
```

Without the ``subPath`` option, the whole structure would be mounted inside the container.
With the ``subPath`` option, only the subset of files/directories anchored at the given subpath gets mounted inside the container.
I.e. using the example above, ``/var/www/html/`` only contains ``index.html`` due to the use of ``subPath: website``.

Note: in my example above, I used a ``persistentVolumeClaim`` (i.e. a named volume from podman's pov), but this should also work for other volume types (e.g. ``hostPath``)

Just for the record, `subPath` can also be used to mount individual files from a `ConfigMap` as explained [here](https://dev.to/joshduffney/kubernetes-using-configmap-subpaths-to-mount-files-3a1i). It's pretty much the same idea that @fpoirotte mentions but it's worth noting since the behavior may change depending on how you implement it. 

An important use case is when you want to add a configuration file inside a container folder that has other default files you want to maintain. Without `subPath`, the whole `ConfigMap` volume is bind mounted on top of the folder and any default configs are left hidden. Unfortunately this is the case for many real life containers, so this feature would really improve usability of `play kube`.

Following the above's example, this kube file should work as expected, leaving default available sites untouched:
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: my-config
data:
  file1.conf: |
    foo=bar
  file2.conf: |
    baz=qux
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: my-lamp-website
spec:
    containers:
    - name: php
      image: docker.io/library/php:7.0-apache
      volumeMounts:
      - mountPath: /etc/apache2/sites-available/my-site.conf
        name: website-config
        subPath: my-site.conf
    volumes:
    - name: website-config
      configMap:
        name: my-config
        items:
         - key: file1.conf
         - path: my-site.conf
```

This is probably the only remaining feature that will make `docker compose` obsolete. With `subPath` we can finally have *all* our configuration inside a single Kubernetes YAML. Looking forward to it!
